source_pr.title: planner: don't push down topn to nil table plan side
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/23965

Problem Summary:

Panic caused by pushing topn down to nil table plan side.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

Check if the order by clause contains no column at all before pushing it down.

### Related changes

N/A

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


Side effects

N/A

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Don't push down topn to nil table plan side

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select extract(day_hour from 'ziy') as res from t order by res limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select extract(day_hour from 'ziy') as res from t order by res limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select extract(day_hour from 'ziy') as res from t order by res limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select extract(day_hour from 'ziy') as res from t order by res limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select extract(day_hour from 'ziy') as res from t order by res limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows()) (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)
